blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha! That's so like Shirone. Why is the letter so long?
It was a rare sight to see his son smiling so brightly at home.
Finally, declaring his defeat, he lowered the newspaper and asked Iruki,

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Is that the kid you mentioned?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Yes, he sent me a letter. He writes very well.
If you're that close, invite him to your house. He's an Unlocker, I'd like to meet him sometime.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: …Allright. I'll talk to him about it.
Iruki's answer lacked enthusiasm.
Introducing Shirone wasn't the problem, but introducing his eccentric father to his friends was a huge issue.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: 'In six days, Father.'
The central city of Sadia is the region where most of Thormia's local nobles reside.
As a famous granary, its finances were solid, and while external forces were few, internal competition for territory was fierce, causing the hierarchy to change frequently.
The West Family, to which Nade belonged, was no exception.
During the break, Nade practically lived in his workshop.
His eyes were hollow after staying up for two nights straight.
The workshop looked like an extension of the Supernatural Psychic Science Research Group.
Tools of unknown use rolled on the floor, and engine parts of some mechanism lay on the table.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Phew, should I call it a day?
Though the season had turned to autumn, the workshop was stuffy.
When he took off his shirt, scars crisscrossing his back were revealed.
It was an old wound, but sometimes it stung so much that it's unbearable.
Nade pulled a tarp over the table.
If his family found out, there would be an uproar, saying he was spending money on something useless.
Though the family had fallen, just three generations ago, half of the Sadia granary belonged to the West Family.
Nade never learned the reason for their decline.
However, after living for 17 years, he felt there wasn't any special reason.
They had simply spent it all.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Nade, nade.
Returning to the mansion, he heard a voice that made his heart stop.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Yes, Mother.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: You said you were good with machines, right? Go to the Jones Family and fix something for them.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Let me just take a quick nap and then I'll go.
His mother's expression twisted horribly like a monster's.
It was like a lock that made it impossible for Nade to move.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: When he was young, he even talked back to his mother.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: But she instead do self-harm, he became the world's greatest delinquent.
You can't win against a mother who harms herself.
No child can win against their parents.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: I'm just tired. I haven't slept in two days.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: What do you evendo? You don't go out and earn money, nor do you help around the house.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: I didn't tell you not to sleep, so why can't you go?
Nade clutched at his distorted facial muscles.
He didn't want to end up with the same face as his mother.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Do you not see how hard your mother working?

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Do you know how much it costs to send my son to that prestigious magic academy?

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: If you're going to be like this, just disappear from this house.
I don't think this mother can support you anymore.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Neid tried to remember when she had ever supported him.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: But there were no such memories.
Only the excruciating scars on his back ached.
No one in West Family ever tried to start even a little business with their wealth.
They did nothing because they never knew how to do anything.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Mom. I understand. I'll go.
The burning pain from Nade's scars was radiating.
But he couldn't show it.
The family couldn't defy her.
No, they avoided her because they didn't want to deal with her.
Then, on days when she commits self-harm, all the family's frustration is poured out on Nade.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: When he was young, he didn't understand why it had to be him.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: But as he grew old, he realized that there is no such thing as a reason.
Nade's family put blame on everything to others and they knew he was the only one who couldn't do the same.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hurry up and go before sunset.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: The Jones family is looking for someone to check their boiler.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: I think I can graduate soon.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: That damn school is a parasite that just sucks money without teaching anything.
I might as well just die.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Nade wanted to understand her mother.
Marrying into a wealthy family only to see her family squander money would naturally cause her great distress.
But no matter how much he thought about it, he couldn't find a reason why he should become a boiler repairman for them.
As his mother returned to the mansion, hot breath escaped from between Nade's teeth.
He felt like murderous intent was seeping into each cell of his body.
Just a little more... if it built just a little more, and he would explode.

blocks.speaker: Butler
blocks.content: Young master, there's a letter... Eek!
The butler who had been running towards him turned pale.
Only then did Nade come to his senses and shake his head.
The second wave of pain hit him as he realized that his mother's face was embedded within him.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Ah, I haven't slept. What's the matter?

blocks.speaker: Butler
blocks.content: A- A letter … It's from Arian Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: What? Shirone?
Nade's face brightened immediately.
Snatching the letter, he began to read Shirone's handwriting.
The moment he saw the phrase 'I miss you,' his heart swelled with sadness and tears welled up in his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Just wait a bit, Shirone! I'll be there soon!
Nade clutched the letter and left the mansion.
The Jones Family wouldn't need a boiler repairman for the next ten years.
The main house of Seriel's family, Fortress, was in Creas City, just like Amy's.
Although their noble rank was not very high, they were a prestigious family known for producing many great doctors, and they were well recognized in the kingdom.

At school, she is Amy's best friend and a model student, but at home, she is a wild and unruly brat who puts her younger brother, who is an aspiring doctor, take the front while she stayed in the background.
Today, she was hiding for the third day to avoid her mother's nagging.
Her younger brother's room, who was expected to become a great doctor in the future, was a nagging-free area where her family didn't go.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Uwaah! What do I do? It's so moving.
Seriel, who was reading a romance novel under the covers, couldn't hold back her tears any longer and started crying.

blocks.speaker: Vale
blocks.content: Ugh, be quiet! I'm trying to study! Go back to your room!

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: It was such a sad love story. Oh, where can I find a man like Joseph?
Vale paused his writing and looked up.

blocks.speaker: Vale
blocks.content: There is.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Really? Who is it?

blocks.speaker: Vale
blocks.content: Joseph.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: You're asking for a beating! Are you teasing your sister?

blocks.speaker: Vale
blocks.content: Why not? If there's no one like him in reality, you might as well live with a fictional character, kekek.
Seriel glared at her brother's back, her lips twitching.
She hadn't gotten angry because she had been indebted to him for a few days, but now he was trying to climb all the way to the top of her head.
Just then, as the door opened, a letter fell out with a thud, along with his mother's nagging.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: You've got a letter! I won't nag you to study, so come out and stop bothering your brother.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Huh? It's Shirone! Shirone!
Seriel quickly opened the envelope and read the letter.
Her eyes instantly filled with affection.
She smelled the scent of the paper and held the letter in her arms, murmuring.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Ah, where can I find a man like Shirone?
Vale's eyes, which had been diligently reading a book, became wide open.

blocks.speaker: Vale
blocks.content: Shirone is a real person, you idiot. Just go date him.
Creas City Municipal Training Ground.
Mark paid the expensive entrance fee and entered the training ground.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Hey, Maria.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: You're here? You're earlier than yesterday.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Sorry. My father noticed. He asked why I'm going out to train when there are good teachers at the Magic Association.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: That's right. The Slider Family is deeply involved with Magic Association.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: No problem. I'm a man of my word.
Mark winked and gave a thumbs up.
In fact, he had barely escaped after starving until morning.
At some point, the fifteen-year-old boy began to see his nineteen-year-old sister as a woman.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: 'I'm crazy, really crazy. To think I'm bullying such a cute sister.'

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Hey! Have you been waiting long? Sorry, I'm late.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Sorry to keep you waiting.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: No, we just got here.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Yeah, I was seeing off the principal, so I'm a bit late.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Oh, where did the principal go?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Well, there are some matters.
Mark felt uncomfortable being left out of the conversation.
He had experienced Sade's personality of being a womanizer enough, but he was particularly affectionate towards Maria, which irked him.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Oh, right! Have you seen this? There's a letter from Senior Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Huh? Senior Shirone? I didn't get one.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: You probably did. I received mine on the way out. I haven't read it yet.
Maria proudly waved the envelope and then opened the letter to read it.
Her expression softened as she read the affectionate content.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Ah, he's such a wonderful person. Strong yet kind. How lucky is Senior Amy to be dating Senior Shirone?
There was a strange tension on Sade's face.
To be honest, Sade was about seventy percent sure that Maria was completely smitten with him.
But hearing this made him think it might just be admiration.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Haha! Yes, Shirone is impressive. But don't most people prefer older partners?

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: If not, at least the same age. Younger men are no fun, right?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Where did that come from?

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: They say love knows no borders, so what does age have to do with it?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Age is more important than borders.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Should we ask Maria? Which would you choose, an older or younger partner?

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Huhu, who know?
Mark and Sade, who had high expectation, were disappointed.
Perhaps she was the true master of push and pull.
Regardless, they couldn't just chat after paying such an expensive entrance fee.
Mark and Maria began their serious training by warming up their minds with Sequencing.
Meanwhile, Sade looked up at the sky and thought of Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: 'The break is almost over. What kind of trouble will he cause when he returns?'
The path that Shirone has taken since entering the magic academy has clearly been unique compared to the history of the academy.
But that's why he was anxious.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: It depends on what kind of answer the principal gives, but if he approves, it will be completely different from Shirone's academy life up to now.
It won't be easy this time, Shirone.
